spike_name: insert_ms cost decomposition + COPY BINARY verdict

context.rationale: TA-11 prelim showed bulk-insert mechanic is only 13% of LUBM-1 insert_ms (UNLOGGED flat = 0.40 us/triple vs LUBM-1 = 3.0 us/triple). TA-10 decomposes the remaining 87% by adding cost components one at a time: WAL, partition routing, hexastore index maintenance. Whichever component dominates is what COPY BINARY (TA-10's spec target) would need to address.

fixtures.name: ta10-C-logged-3-indexes
fixtures.path: LOGGED + SPO/POS/OSP hexastore indexes (mechanic + WAL + indexes)

default.delta_attribution: real-data variability + partitioned-AND-indexed combination (TA-10 spike tested either OR — not both)

cost_decomposition_lubm1_insert_ms.note: Percentages derived from variant deltas / 312ms total LUBM-1 insert_ms. Index maintenance is the dominant single component.

verdict: TA-10 (COPY BINARY) — like TA-11 (heap_multi_insert) — does NOT address the dominant cost component (hexastore index maintenance at ~51% of LUBM-1 insert_ms). COPY BINARY routes through PG's INSERT machinery and triggers the same per-row index maintenance. Theoretical ceiling at LUBM-1 scale: ~5-10% improvement (same order as heap_multi_insert). For the implementation effort (~200 lines of unsafe Rust against pgrx::pg_sys CopyFrom + binary tuple-encoding), this is poor ROI compared to TA-D3 (-17%) and TA-D2 (-54%) already shipped. **Recommended: TA-10 MEASURED NOT WORTH IMPLEMENTING (same verdict as TA-11).** The real Track A insert-path lever is HEXASTORE INDEX MAINTENANCE; possible future Track-A item: bulk-ingest mode that drops indexes, batches inserts, rebuilds indexes (classic PG bulk-load pattern). Out of scope for current Track A; recommend filing as TA-NEW-Z for v0.7+ consideration.

track_a_spike_chain_summary.ta_11_heap_multi_insert: MEASURED NOT WORTH IMPLEMENTING (4% ceiling) — shipped v0.5.32 as prelim
track_a_spike_chain_summary.ta_10_copy_binary: MEASURED NOT WORTH IMPLEMENTING (5-10% ceiling, same root cause as TA-11) — this spike, ships v0.5.33 as prelim
track_a_spike_chain_summary.real_insert_path_bottleneck: Hexastore index maintenance — not addressed by any of the spec'd Track A spikes
track_a_spike_chain_summary.next_action: TA-D1 (dict-path decision) + TA-9 (insert-path decision integrating TA-D1). Both spike chains' decisions inform TA-7 (production landing).
